name: Employee Benefits
text: The Employee shall be entitled to the following benefits during the term of employment: (a) Provident Fund contributions as required under the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952, with both the Employer and Employee contributing at the applicable statutory rates; (b) Employee State Insurance contributions as required under the Employees' State Insurance Act, 1948, where applicable; (c) Gratuity upon completion of five (5) years of continuous service as required under the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972; (d) Earned Leave of eighteen (18) days per calendar year; (e) Sick Leave of twelve (12) days per calendar year; (f) Public holidays as notified by the Central Government and the applicable State Government; and (g) such other benefits as the Employer may specify in its HR Policy, as amended from time to time. The Employer shall maintain all statutory registers and make all prescribed returns as required under applicable labour laws.

legal_basis.act: Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952

invalid_if: PF contributions below statutory minimum of 12% of basic wages
Earned leave entitlement below minimum prescribed under applicable state Shops and Establishments Act
source: Employees' Provident Funds Act, 1952; Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972
